title: Kedarkantha Trek
description: Kedarkantha is one of the most beloved winter treks in India, offering stunning snow-clad meadows and 360-degree panoramic views of the Garhwal Himalaya. The trek passes through dense pine forests, quaint villages, and magical frozen lakes. A perfect blend of adventure and natural beauty, this trek is ideal for beginners and seasoned trekkers alike.

itinerary.title: Dehradun to Sankri
itinerary.description: Depart from Dehradun early morning and drive approximately 200 km to Sankri village (6,400 ft). The scenic drive takes you through Mussoorie and Purola along the Tons River valley. Check into the guesthouse and attend a trek briefing in the evening.

itinerary.title: Sankri to Juda Ka Talab
itinerary.description: Begin your trek from Sankri through dense pine and oak forests to reach Juda Ka Talab (9,100 ft). The trail offers beautiful views of the surrounding peaks. The lake here often freezes in winter, creating a magical landscape.

itinerary.title: Juda Ka Talab to Kedarkantha Base Camp
itinerary.description: Continue through increasingly snow-covered trails to reach the Kedarkantha Base Camp (11,250 ft). The campsite sits in a clearing surrounded by towering pine trees dusted with snow. Acclimatize and prepare for the summit push next morning.

itinerary.title: Summit Day – Kedarkantha Peak & Descent to Hargaon
itinerary.description: Start early for the summit push to Kedarkantha Peak (12,500 ft). The sunrise from the top reveals a breathtaking panorama of peaks including Swargarohini, Bandarpoonch, Kala Nag, and Ranglana. Descend to Hargaon camp after the summit.

itinerary.title: Hargaon to Sankri
itinerary.description: Descend through beautiful forest trails back to Sankri. The trail offers different perspectives of the mountains you explored. Arrive at Sankri in the afternoon and celebrate your successful trek.

itinerary.title: Sankri to Dehradun
itinerary.description: After breakfast, drive back to Dehradun (approximately 8-9 hours). Stop at Mussoorie for lunch if time permits. Arrive at Dehradun by evening with wonderful memories of your Himalayan adventure.

included: All meals during the trek (Day 1 dinner to Day 6 breakfast)
Experienced and certified trek guide
Trekking permits and forest fees
Quality camping equipment (tents, sleeping bags rated to -10°C)
First aid kit and emergency oxygen cylinder
Transport from Dehradun to Sankri and back
Porters/mules for carrying camping equipment
excluded: Any meals outside the trek itinerary
Rescue charges in case of emergency evacuation
highlights: 360-degree panoramic views of 13 Himalayan peaks from the summit
Walk through snow-covered meadows and pine forests
Camp under a starlit Himalayan sky
Visit the frozen Juda Ka Talab lake
Perfect introductory winter high-altitude trek
Witness magical winter sunrises over the Garhwal Himalaya

title: Valley of Flowers Trek
tagline: UNESCO World Heritage of floral paradise
description: The Valley of Flowers is a UNESCO World Heritage Site nestled in the West Himalayan region of Uttarakhand. Carpeted with hundreds of species of wildflowers, including rare and endangered Himalayan flora, the valley comes alive from July to September. Combined with a visit to the revered Hemkund Sahib shrine, this trek offers both natural wonder and spiritual enrichment.

itinerary.title: Haridwar / Rishikesh to Govindghat
itinerary.description: Drive from Haridwar or Rishikesh to Govindghat (6,000 ft), the base for both Valley of Flowers and Hemkund Sahib. The journey takes approximately 10 hours through scenic Chamoli district. Check in and prepare for the trek ahead.

itinerary.title: Govindghat to Ghangaria
itinerary.description: Trek 13 km from Govindghat to Ghangaria (10,000 ft) along the Pushpawati River. The path is well-maintained and passes through dense forests and small waterfalls. Ghangaria serves as the base camp for both Valley of Flowers and Hemkund Sahib.

itinerary.title: Ghangaria to Valley of Flowers and Back
itinerary.description: Explore the magical Valley of Flowers (11,500-14,100 ft) for a full day. Walk through the 87.5 sq km valley carpeted with over 500 species of wildflowers. Spot rare flora like Brahma Kamal, Blue Poppy, and various orchids. Return to Ghangaria by evening.

itinerary.title: Ghangaria to Hemkund Sahib and Back
itinerary.description: Ascend 6 km to Hemkund Sahib (14,100 ft), a sacred Sikh shrine situated beside a glacial lake surrounded by seven snow-clad peaks. Take a holy dip in the pristine lake and visit the gurudwara. Descend back to Ghangaria in the afternoon.

itinerary.title: Ghangaria to Govindghat
itinerary.description: Trek back down to Govindghat through the same scenic trail. Enjoy the views one final time before returning to the valley. Rest and recover at Govindghat.

itinerary.title: Govindghat to Haridwar
itinerary.description: Drive back from Govindghat to Haridwar or Rishikesh. Stop at Badrinath Temple if time permits for blessings. Arrive at Haridwar by evening carrying memories of the floral paradise.

included: All meals during the trek (Day 1 dinner to Day 6 breakfast)
Experienced certified trek guide and local naturalist
National Park entry fees and Valley of Flowers permit
Accommodation in guesthouses/camps throughout
First aid kit and emergency medicines
Transport from Haridwar to Govindghat and back
Porters for carrying group equipment
excluded: Personal trekking gear and rain gear
Any extra days due to weather or personal reasons
highlights: UNESCO World Heritage Site with 500+ species of wildflowers
Visit the sacred Hemkund Sahib Gurudwara at 14,100 ft
Spot rare Himalayan flora including Brahma Kamal and Blue Poppy
Walk alongside the crystal-clear Pushpawati River
Witness stunning biodiversity unique to the western Himalayas
Comfortable well-established trail suitable for all fitness levels
meetingPoint: Haridwar Railway Station / Rishikesh Bus Stand

title: Roopkund Trek
description: Roopkund Trek leads to the mysterious skeleton lake at 15,696 ft, surrounded by glaciers and the imposing Trishul and Nanda Ghunti peaks. The lake holds hundreds of ancient human skeletal remains, estimated to be over 1,200 years old, making it one of the most intriguing high-altitude destinations in the world. This challenging trek rewards you with some of the most spectacular mountain scenery in the Garhwal Himalaya.

itinerary.title: Kathgodam / Haridwar to Lohajung
itinerary.description: Drive from Kathgodam or Haridwar to Lohajung (7,700 ft), the base camp for Roopkund Trek. The drive takes approximately 10-12 hours through the beautiful Kumaon hills. Arrive at Lohajung, check in, and attend a detailed briefing about the trek.

itinerary.title: Lohajung to Didna Village
itinerary.description: Trek from Lohajung through meadows and forests to Didna Village (8,200 ft). The trail offers beautiful views of Ali Bugyal ahead. This is a gradual trek helping you acclimatize to the altitude.

itinerary.title: Didna to Ali Bugyal
itinerary.description: Ascend through steep forest trails to reach the spectacular Ali Bugyal (11,900 ft), one of the largest high-altitude meadows in Asia. The meadow provides panoramic views of Nanda Ghunti, Trishul, and other peaks.

itinerary.title: Ali Bugyal to Patar Nachauni
itinerary.description: Continue across the stunning bugyal to reach Patar Nachauni (12,700 ft). The trail crosses gentle slopes with increasingly dramatic mountain vistas. The campsite here offers excellent views of the surrounding peaks.

itinerary.title: Patar Nachauni to Bhagwabasa
itinerary.description: Trek to Bhagwabasa (14,100 ft), the last campsite before Roopkund. The terrain becomes more barren and rocky. This is a critical acclimatization stop before the final push to the lake.

itinerary.title: Bhagwabasa to Roopkund Lake and Back to Patar Nachauni
itinerary.description: The most challenging and rewarding day – ascend to the mysterious Roopkund Lake (15,696 ft). Witness the ancient skeletal remains in the frozen lake and soak in the magnificent views of Trishul (23,360 ft) and Nanda Ghunti. Descend to Patar Nachauni.

itinerary.title: Patar Nachauni to Wan Village
itinerary.description: Descend via a different route through Bedni Bugyal and Wan Village (8,000 ft). This alternative descent route offers new perspectives of the landscape and a chance to explore traditional Garhwali village life.

itinerary.title: Wan to Kathgodam / Haridwar
itinerary.description: Drive from Wan to Kathgodam or Haridwar. The journey takes approximately 8-10 hours. Arrive at your destination by evening with incredible memories of the mystical Roopkund.

included: All meals during the trek (Day 1 dinner to Day 8 breakfast)
Experienced certified high-altitude trek guide
All trekking permits and Nanda Devi Biosphere Reserve fees
High-quality expedition camping equipment
First aid kit, emergency oxygen, and satellite phone
Transport from Haridwar/Kathgodam to Lohajung and back
Porters and mules for equipment
Emergency evacuation support
excluded: Personal high-altitude trekking gear (boots, crampons, ice axe)
highlights: Visit the mysterious skeleton lake filled with 1,200-year-old remains
Panoramic views of Trishul (23,360 ft) and Nanda Ghunti peaks
Walk across Ali and Bedni Bugyal, Asia's largest high-altitude meadows
Experience true high-altitude trekking above 15,000 ft
Explore traditional Garhwali villages and culture
Witness dramatic landscape transitions from forests to glaciers
meetingPoint: Kathgodam Railway Station / Haridwar Railway Station

title: Har Ki Dun Trek
tagline: Valley of the Gods in the Himalayas
description: Har Ki Dun, meaning 'Valley of Gods', is a cradle-shaped hanging valley in the Garhwal Himalayas of Uttarakhand. According to Hindu mythology, this is the route taken by the Pandavas on their journey to heaven. The trek offers breathtaking views of Swargarohini peaks, lush green meadows, dense forests of oak and rhododendron, and ancient villages that have preserved their traditional way of life for centuries.

itinerary.title: Dehradun to Sankri
itinerary.description: Drive from Dehradun to Sankri (6,400 ft) through the picturesque Tons Valley. The journey takes about 9-10 hours. Sankri is a beautiful village surrounded by pine forests. Check in and prepare for the trek.

itinerary.title: Sankri to Taluka
itinerary.description: Drive from Sankri to Taluka (5,900 ft) and begin the trek to Seema Village (7,100 ft). The trail follows the Supin River through dense oak and rhododendron forests. The river crossings add to the adventure.

itinerary.title: Seema to Osla
itinerary.description: Continue through the valley to the ancient village of Osla (9,500 ft). This traditional village retains its old wooden architecture and the famous Duryodhana Temple, one of the few temples dedicated to the Kaurava prince.

itinerary.title: Osla to Har Ki Dun
itinerary.description: The final ascent to Har Ki Dun valley (11,675 ft). The trail opens up to reveal the magnificent cradle-shaped valley with stunning views of Swargarohini, Hata Peak, and Jaundhar Glacier. Spend the afternoon exploring this mythological valley.

itinerary.title: Har Ki Dun Exploration Day
itinerary.description: Full day to explore Har Ki Dun. Optional side trek to Jaundhar Glacier. Enjoy the spectacular mountain scenery, spot Himalayan wildlife, and immerse yourself in the mythological significance of this sacred valley.

itinerary.title: Har Ki Dun to Seema
itinerary.description: Begin the return journey, descending through the same beautiful trail. The descent offers different perspectives of the valley. Overnight at Seema village.

itinerary.title: Seema to Taluka, Drive to Dehradun
itinerary.description: Trek back to Taluka and drive to Dehradun. Arrive by evening with memories of the legendary Valley of Gods.

included: All meals during the trek (Day 1 dinner to Day 7 breakfast)
Experienced local trek guide with deep knowledge of the region
All forest permits and camping fees
Quality tents and sleeping bags
First aid kit and emergency medicines
Transport from Dehradun to Sankri/Taluka and back
All camping equipment and kitchen gear
excluded: Any expenses arising from unforeseen circumstances
highlights: Trek through the mythological Valley of Gods (Pandava route to heaven)
Visit the unique Duryodhana Temple in Osla village
Panoramic views of Swargarohini peaks and Jaundhar Glacier
Explore authentic traditional Garhwali village architecture
Rich biodiversity with Himalayan flora and fauna
Walk through dense rhododendron and oak forests

title: Brahmatal Trek
tagline: Winter trek with frozen alpine lakes
description: Brahmatal Trek is a hidden gem of the Garhwal Himalayas, offering stunning views of Mt. Trishul and Nanda Ghunti from atop the Brahmatal Pass. The trail winds through snow-covered meadows and frozen lakes during winter months, creating a magical white landscape. According to Hindu mythology, Lord Brahma meditated at the Brahmatal Lake, giving the trek its sacred name.

itinerary.title: Haridwar to Lohajung
itinerary.description: Drive from Haridwar to Lohajung (7,700 ft), the base camp for Brahmatal Trek. The scenic 12-hour drive takes you through Chamoli district. Lohajung is a beautiful mountain village offering initial views of the high Himalayan peaks.

itinerary.title: Lohajung to Bekaltal
itinerary.description: Start trekking from Lohajung through oak and rhododendron forests to Bekaltal (9,600 ft). This small alpine lake surrounded by forest is often frozen in winter, creating a picturesque campsite.

itinerary.title: Bekaltal to Brahmatal
itinerary.description: Continue uphill through deep snow to the sacred Brahmatal Lake (10,800 ft). The trail offers increasingly spectacular views. The frozen lake with snow-dusted trees creates an ethereal winter landscape.

itinerary.title: Brahmatal Pass Summit and Return
itinerary.description: Summit day to Brahmatal Pass (12,250 ft). The panoramic views of Trishul (23,360 ft) and Nanda Ghunti (19,100 ft) from the top are absolutely breathtaking. Return to Brahmatal Lake for overnight camping.

itinerary.title: Brahmatal to Lohajung
itinerary.description: Descend back through the snow-covered trail to Lohajung. Enjoy the beautiful winter landscapes one final time as you descend.

itinerary.title: Lohajung to Haridwar
itinerary.description: Drive back to Haridwar arriving in the evening. Carry the memories of frozen lakes, snowy meadows, and magnificent Himalayan peaks back home.

included: All meals during the trek (Day 1 dinner to Day 6 breakfast)
Expert winter trek guide
All permits and camping fees
Winter-grade camping equipment (sleeping bags rated -15°C)
First aid and emergency oxygen
Transport from Haridwar to Lohajung and back
Snow equipment assistance
excluded: Personal winter trekking gear and gaiters
Travel insurance (strongly recommended for winter treks)
Any costs arising from weather delays
highlights: Stunning views of Trishul and Nanda Ghunti peaks
Walk through pristine snow-covered meadows and forests
Camp beside the sacred frozen Brahmatal Lake
Mythological significance – Lord Brahma's meditation site
Excellent winter photography opportunities
Off-the-beaten-path trek with fewer crowds
meetingPoint: Haridwar Railway Station / Rishikesh Bus Stand

title: Pangarchulla Peak Expedition
description: Pangarchulla Peak Expedition is the ideal 'first peak' for aspiring mountaineers, offering a genuine high-altitude climbing experience at 14,700 ft. The expedition combines trekking through the famous Kuari Pass trail with technical snow and ice climbing on the final ascent. With spectacular views of Nanda Devi, Dronagiri, Kamet, and Hathi-Ghoda peaks, this is an unforgettable adventure for those seeking to step beyond trekking into mountaineering.

itinerary.title: Haridwar to Joshimath
itinerary.description: Drive from Haridwar to Joshimath (6,200 ft), the gateway to many high Himalayan destinations. The 10-hour drive takes you through Devprayag and Chamoli. Joshimath offers acclimatization and the first views of high Himalayan peaks.

itinerary.title: Joshimath to Dhak Village to Gulling Top
itinerary.description: Drive to Dhak village and begin trekking to Gulling Top (10,200 ft). The trail passes through beautiful forests and offers views of Auli ski resort. Acclimatize at the campsite.

itinerary.title: Gulling Top to Tali
itinerary.description: Continue trekking to Tali campsite (11,800 ft) through the Kuari Pass trail. The views of Nanda Devi, Dronagiri, and Kamet begin to dominate the skyline. The trail crosses open meadows with wildflowers.

itinerary.title: Tali to Khullara (Base Camp)
itinerary.description: Reach Khullara (12,800 ft), the base camp for Pangarchulla Peak. This is the most critical acclimatization day. Practice crampon techniques and rope management under guide supervision in the afternoon.

itinerary.title: Summit Day – Pangarchulla Peak
itinerary.description: Early morning alpine start for the summit push. The final 2,000 ft involves steep snow and ice slopes requiring crampons and ice axe. The summit views of Nanda Devi (25,643 ft), Dronagiri, Kamet, Mana, and Hathi-Ghoda peaks are magnificent. Descend to Khullara.

itinerary.title: Khullara to Auli
itinerary.description: Descend from base camp to the famous Auli ski resort (8,200 ft). The trail offers panoramic views of the peaks you conquered. Celebrate your summit success at Auli.

itinerary.title: Auli / Joshimath to Haridwar
itinerary.description: Drive back to Haridwar from Joshimath. Arrive in the evening with the proud achievement of having climbed your first Himalayan peak.

included: All meals during the expedition (Day 1 dinner to Day 7 breakfast)
Certified mountaineering guide with high-altitude experience
All permits, peak fees, and forest department charges
Technical mountaineering equipment (crampons, ice axe, ropes, harness)
Expedition-grade camping equipment
First aid kit, emergency oxygen, and satellite communication
Transport from Haridwar to Joshimath and back
Pre-expedition basic mountaineering training
excluded: Personal mountaineering gear and high-altitude clothing
Any costs due to weather-related delays or failed summit
highlights: Stand atop Pangarchulla Peak with views of Nanda Devi and Kamet
Experience technical mountaineering with crampons and ice axe
Follow the famous Kuari Pass trail through Nanda Devi Sanctuary
Perfect introduction to Himalayan mountaineering
360-degree panorama including 8 major Himalayan peaks
Professional mountaineering training included in the program
